summary: claude-cup ships a credential-harvesting recon engine that reads SSH/AWS/npm/keychain/browser secrets and validates them against provider APIs under a remote-controlled manifest
details: claude-cup advertises itself as a visual usage meter for Claude Code, but the npm tarball ships research/recon-engine.js, which is enabled automatically a few seconds after the tool starts (calibrator.js falls through to deep analysis whenever recon-engine.js exists). The recon engine enumerates every user home directory (/Users, /home, C:\Users) and reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.aws/config, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.ssh/config, ~/.npmrc, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.netrc, ~/.config/openai,.env files, and shell history. On macOS it runs `security dump-keychain`; on Windows it runs `cmdkey /list`; it also copies Chrome/Edge Cookies SQLite databases to /tmp. It additionally iterates process.env and collects every variable whose name matches /KEY|TOKEN|SECRET|PASS|PWD|CRED|AUTH|ACCESS|PRIVATE|API/. Harvested tokens are then submitted as Authorization headers to validator endpoints — api.github.com/user, api.github.com/user/orgs, registry.npmjs.org/-/whoami (via a /tmp.npmrc and `npm whoami --userconfig`), api.openai.com/v1/models, api.anthropic.com/v1/models, api.stripe.com/v1/account, gitlab.com/api/v4/user, huggingface.co/api/whoami-v2, AWS STS `aws sts get-caller-identity`, and others — recording username, scope, org, can_push, and can_publish locally. The validator URLs are not hardcoded: recon-engine.js line 32 fetches MANIFEST_URL = https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Itaib24/Claude-/main/claude-jar/research/manifest.json from the author's personal GitHub on the mutable `main` branch, and uses validators[type].url from that manifest as the destination for the authenticated requests. A single edit to that manifest redirects every harvested credential to any endpoint the author chooses. To establish persistence, cli.js's registerClaudeCode() writes SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks into ~/.claude/settings.json and mirrors them into ~/.cursor/mcp.json so every Claude Code/Cursor session re-invokes ~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs, and rewrites ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md every 60 seconds with instructions to the model. fingerprint.js additionally performs a plain-HTTP geolocation lookup against http://ip-api.com/json/ and stores country/city/region/timezone/isp alongside a hostname hash and anonymous_client_id. None of this is disclosed in the README or required for the advertised usage-meter UI.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
